comment: pacifist-bot band for --sim 5000 --policy pacifist (make sim, batch 5 T2/addendum, DECISION.md item 3 — the pacifist band gate mercy's fate hinges on). Re-baselined 2026-07-19 for the parley addendum (human direction: 'needs to be algorithm'd'), which replaces T1's flat guaranteed-stayed-swing counter with a receptivity-rolled talk (game::receptivity, rolled against a new parley_rng channel). Formula and constants used for this measurement, all left at the addendum's starting values (no tuning was needed — the first measurement already landed inside the required band): receptivity = BASE[kind] (rat 55, goblin 35, ogre 20) + 18*regard + 40*(maxhp-hp)/maxhp + 6*(atk-3) - 10 if fov_radius(light)<=4, clamped to [5,95]; landed roll (parley_rng.range(0,100) < receptivity) advances regard by 1 and stays the monster that turn (existing stayed-swing mechanism, now conditional on landing); failed roll advances nothing and does NOT stay the monster, so it can retaliate that same turn. talk_threshold unchanged (rat 2, goblin 3, ogre 4). Measured: wins 394/5000 (7.88%, win_pct 7 integer), deaths_combat 4599 (91.98%), deaths_dark 7 (0.14%), stuck 0, kills_total 0 (pacifist_never_attacks holds), spared_total 38073, median win turns 1321 (vs greedy's 1258), median light left on win 358, min light left on win 10. Greedy control re-run same session, UNCHANGED and exact: wins 726/5000 (14.5%), deaths_combat 4266, deaths_dark 8, stuck 0 (tests/sim-band.json unmoved, proving the new parley_rng channel does not leak into greedy's path — greedy never talks). FINDING: the parley roll reverses the batch-5-T1 inversion finding this file previously recorded. Under the old flat/guaranteed mechanic pacifist won MORE than greedy (22.8% vs 14.5%) because every talk unconditionally stayed its target, removing a full turn of retaliation risk on every single encounter. Under the addendum's roll, a failed talk removes that protection — the monster is not stayed and can attack the same turn the persuasion attempt fails — so failed talks now carry real risk, exactly as the addendum's design intent states ('failed talks carry risk'). deaths_combat rose from 3841 to 4599 of 5000 (76.8% to 92.0%) and win_pct fell from 22.8% to 7.88% as a direct consequence: mercy is now the harder path again, consistent with the ORIGINAL batch-2 finding this whole mercy arc set out to address, but now via a probabilistic cost (a bad roll can get you hit) rather than an absolute wall (guaranteed combat lethality with no mercy option at all). Required shape per the addendum: win_pct floor >= 5, cap <= 40 (mercy must be viable but must not dominate). The measured 7 sits close to the mandated floor by construction (receptivity's BASE table and +18 regard coefficient were left at their starting values because the very first measurement already cleared the floor with no tuning), not because it was pushed there under stress; the [5,20] band still has headroom to catch a large drift toward greedy-style dominance (a jump well past the measured value). Band is calibrated for 5000 seeds (smaller runs can trip the floor spuriously, same caveat as sim-band.json). Re-baselining (a knob change to receptivity's constants, talk_threshold, or a becalm light refund) is a balance change: human sign-off required, same as tests/sim-band.json. Sign-off for this measurement: the 2026-07-19 batch-5 addendum itself, which pre-authorized this remeasurement and re-baseline as part of landing the parley roll.
